xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #54

plan.title: Align all fog horns to Foghorn 3's tone interval

narratives.Description: Align all fog horns to Foghorn 3's tone interval
narratives.Overview:
## Request

All fog horns should follow the same tone interval that fog horn 3 now uses.

## Canonical interval (Foghorn 3)

Foghorn 3 was retuned in #50 / PR #51 to **C2 → F1** — a **perfect fifth** (7 semitones down). That interval is the target for the other fog horns.

Defined in `src/audio/voices.ts` as `FOG_HORN_3_INTERVAL_SEMITONES = 7` (C2 `65.41` Hz → F1 `43.66` Hz), and documented in `src/lib/meditation.ts`.

## Current intervals (as of filing)

| Voice | Current tones | Interval |
|-------|---------------|----------|
| Fog horn 1 | Single B1 (~61.74 Hz) via `distantHorn` | No two-tone interval (sustained single blast) |
| Fog horn 2 | D3 → F♯2/G♭2 | Minor sixth (8 semitones) |
| Fog horn 3 | C2 → F1 | Perfect fifth (7 semitones) — **canonical** |
| Fog horn 4 | C3 → G2 | Perfect fourth (5 semitones) |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fog horns 2 and 4 use a **perfect fifth** (7 semitones) between tone 1 and tone 2, matching Foghorn 3’s interval (absolute pitch may stay voice-specific if desired; the *interval* must match).
- [ ] Fog horn 1 either gains a matching two-tone perfect-fifth pattern, or is explicitly scoped out with rationale if it remains a single-tone distant horn.
- [ ] Constants / comments in `src/audio/voices.ts` and descriptions in `src/lib/meditation.ts` reflect the aligned intervals.
- [ ] Existing voice tuning tests (`src/audio/voices.test.ts`, `src/lib/meditation.test.ts`) are updated accordingly and pass.

## Notes

- Scope is **tone interval** alignment (semitone distance between blasts), not necessarily identical absolute pitches or reverb/timbre.
- Do not change ship/train horns unless they are later found to share the same two-tone fog-horn pattern.
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1: Fog horns 2 and 4 use a perfect fifth (7 semitones) between tone 1 and tone 2; absolute pitch may stay voice-specific.
FR-2: Fog horn 1 converts to a two-tone perfect-fifth pattern (B1 high → E1 low) via gatedTwoBlastHorn, matching the fog-horn family interval semantics.
FR-3: Named Hz / interval constants and comments in src/audio/voices.ts plus catalog descriptions in src/lib/meditation.ts document the aligned perfect-fifth intervals.
FR-4: voices.test.ts and meditation.test.ts cover the new tunings and pass.
NFR-1: Pitch/interval/docs/tests only — no ship/train horn changes; no reverb/timing redesign beyond what converting fog horn 1 to two-tone requires.
narratives.LockedDecisions: Refinement 2026-07-13 (#54 Evaluate lock, operator accept+implement): (1) AC locked as FR-1–4 + NFR-1 from the issue body. (2) Fog horn 1 is IN SCOPE: convert from single-tone distantHorn to two-tone gatedTwoBlastHorn with B1 (61.74 Hz) as tone 1 and E1 (~41.21 Hz) as tone 2 — perfect fifth down; keep B1 as the high of the pair. (3) Fog horn 2 keeps D3 tone 1; tone 2 retunes to G2 (~98.00 Hz). (4) Fog horn 4 keeps C3 tone 1; tone 2 retunes to F2 (~87.31 Hz). (5) Fog horn 3 unchanged (canonical). (6) Out of scope: ship/train horns, announce files, UI beyond catalog descriptions.

items.title: Fog horns 2 and 4 use a **perfect fifth** (7 semitones) between tone 1 and tone 2, matching Foghorn 3’s interval (absolute pitch may stay voice-specific if desired; the *interval* must match).
Fog horn 1 either gains a matching two-tone perfect-fifth pattern, or is explicitly scoped out with rationale if it remains a single-tone distant horn.
Constants / comments in `src/audio/voices.ts` and descriptions in `src/lib/meditation.ts` reflect the aligned intervals.
Existing voice tuning tests (`src/audio/voices.test.ts`, `src/lib/meditation.test.ts`) are updated accordingly and pass.
